hawkish_phrases: CPI inflation is expected to rise quite sharply towards the target in the spring
The Committee does not intend to tighten monetary policy at least until there is clear evidence that significant progress is being made in eliminating spare capacity
Activity has been stronger than expected, despite the recent rise in Covid cases
dovish_phrases: If the outlook for inflation weakens, the Committee stands ready to take whatever additional action is necessary to achieve its remit
Twelve-month CPI inflation fell to 0.3% in November
a substantial further increase is still likely over the next few quarters
consistent with a material degree of spare capacity
The pandemic would weigh on near-term spending to a greater extent than projected
summary: The MPC voted unanimously to maintain Bank Rate at 0.1% and continue its asset purchase programme, with explicit forward guidance standing ready to take additional easing action if the inflation outlook weakens, amid very low inflation (0.3%) and rising unemployment.
